result:
Here's a summary of the changes I made:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
The `format` method in `KtLintRuleEngine` executed rules only once. Due to complex rule execution dependencies (via `Rule.VisitorModifier`), a single pass could not always resolve all auto-correctable format errors. One rule's fix might create a new violation that another rule could fix, but since all rules only ran once, these cascading issues were left unresolved.

### Solution

**Two files modified:**

#### 1. `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/api/KtLintRuleEngine.kt`

- Wrapped the auto-correct format pass in a `do-while` loop that repeats as long as auto-correctable violations are found
- Added a `MAX_FORMAT_RUNS_PER_FILE = 3` constant to cap the maximum number of format passes, preventing infinite loops from rules that never converge
- The loop tracks `autoCorrectIteration` — set to `true` when any auto-correctable violation is found and corrected. If no auto-corrections were made in a pass, the loop terminates early
- After the loop, the final `autoCorrect=false` lint pass still runs (if any violations were found in the last pass) to collect remaining violations that couldn't be auto-corrected
- Added debug logging for each format run iteration

#### 2. `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/StringTemplateIndentRule.kt`

- Fixed `checkAndFixNewLineAfterOpeningQuotes()`: Added check `!firstNodeAfterOpeningQuotes.text.startsWith("\n")` to prevent inserting duplicate newlines after opening quotes on repeated format runs
- Fixed `checkAndFixNewLineBeforeClosingQuotes()`: Added check `!lastNodeBeforeClosingQuotes.text.endsWith("\n" + indent)` to prevent inserting duplicate newlines before closing quotes on repeated format runs

These fixes make the `StringTemplateIndentRule` idempotent — the repeated format loop exposed that this rule was not safe to run multiple times on the same AST.
